function: bloaty::Bloaty::ScanAndRollupFile(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bloaty::Rollup*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > > >*) const
what_input_feature: The leading bytes must form a Mach-O magic constant (ce fa ed fe / cf fa ed fe) followed by enough valid Mach-O load-command/section structure (including __ section-name bytes 5f 5f) that file->GetBuildId() returns a non-empty string, so the true side of if (!build_id.empty()) is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe (or cf fa ed fe) and carry embedded 5f 5f (__) section-name bytes and load-command sizing fields; these parse into a Mach-O whose build_id (UUID) is recovered, populating build_id and exercising lines 1751-1757 only in W.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d (WASM \0asm) or similar non-Mach-O magic; bloaty parses them as a format that yields an empty build_id, so GetBuildId() returns empty and line 1750 always takes the false side (T=0 in L).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the object-file format CMPs (magic and structural-field comparisons) and I2SRandReplace splices the exact required literals (Mach-O magic, section-name bytes) into the input at the offsets where the parser compares them; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct the multi-field magic/structure by blind havoc, so they never produce a parseable build_id.

what_input_feature: The first four bytes must equal the Mach-O magic number ce fa ed fe (little-endian 0xFEEDFACE). Bloaty's format dispatch matches this magic to enter the Mach-O object reader, which calls AddRange / FindContaining repeatedly; without the magic match the input is routed to a different (e.g. WASM) parser whose path drives FindContaining only on the false side.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with ce fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe (and a few ASCII M/Z/L/E/P/F) at those offsets, and the winners reach FindContaining 562 times and its callers far more, with the blocker true side taken (T=101/160).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM magic '.asm'), so the Mach-O reader is never entered; FindContaining is still invoked 1720 times on the L set but the blocker true side is taken 0 times (T=0 F=1720), i.e. the loser parser path never produces the empty-result condition the true branch encodes.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the format-magic comparison and log both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the seed's leading bytes and substitutes the expected magic operand (ce fa ed fe) directly at offset 0. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto the exact 4-byte magic by chance via havoc, which is why naive never resolves (0/10) and value_profile resolves only 1/10.

source_line: case cmsSigXYZData: // Input profile operates in XYZ
what_input_feature: The ICC profile's connection-space signature at byte offset 0x10-0x13 must NOT equal the literal 'XYZ ' (cmsSigXYZData = 0x58595A20). When it spells 'Lab ' (0x4C616220) or another non-XYZ space, the switch on InPCS at line 429 falls through cmsSigXYZData and InPCS takes the false side of the case test, routing into the cmsSigLabData / default arms.
why_winner_satisfies: All winning-resolving seeds carry a non-XYZ PCS literal at 0x10: seed 1/3/5 (cmplog) and seed 2 hold 'Lab' / 'MCH4Lab', and the BYTE DIFF shows W bytes 4c(L)/61(a)/62(b) at offsets 0x10/0x11/0x12 versus L bytes 52(R)/47(G)/42(B). The W per-role overlay reaches the cmsSigLabData arm (lines 454-477) that only executes on the false side of the XYZ case.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 'RGB XYZ' at 0x10-0x17, so InPCS resolves to cmsSigXYZData and the true side of line 429 is taken (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L T=60 F=0 at line 429). The loser never reaches the Lab-input arm and the false branch stays globally blocked.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted profile-signature CMP (the switch on InPCS compiles to integer comparisons against the cmsSigXYZData constant 0x58595A20), finds the matching 'XYZ ' bytes in the input, and substitutes the other observed operand ('Lab '/etc.) at offset 0x10, directly flipping the PCS field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reliably synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC at that offset; value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback gives no monotone path to a discrete FOURCC literal, so both losers stall.

source_line: case cmsSigLabData: // Input profile operates in Lab
what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace/PCS signature in the profile header (offset 0x10 in these seeds) must be the FOURCC 'Lab ' = 0x4c 0x61 0x62 0x20, so InPCS in AddConversion's switch equals cmsSigLabData and the true side at line 452 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at offset 0x10-0x13, matching the cmsSigLabData switch constant; the per-role overlay then enters the Lab-input arm (lines 454-478) marked [W].
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offset 0x10, an RGB/non-PCS device colorspace; InPCS therefore never equals cmsSigLabData and the case-452 true side is never taken (loser hitcount on branch = 0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the switch comparison against the cmsSigLabData literal, records the constant 'Lab ' in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes it into the input at the offset where the prior operand ('RGB ' / other signature) appeared; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot directly plant the exact 4-byte magic, so they stall on the false side.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature at input offset 0x14 must be the FOURCC 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) while the PCS/colorspace field at 0x10 reads 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20), so the two compared colorspace signatures equal cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData respectively, satisfying the compound equality gate at line 502.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal FOURCC bytes that make a==cmsSigXYZData && b==cmsSigLabData; the per-role overlay shows the true side of line 502 taken 20 times in W (T=20) and the on-branch hitcount is 121-187, whereas loser seeds never take it.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at 0x10 and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) at 0x14 in the opposite slots, so a==cmsSigRGBData not cmsSigXYZData; line 502's true side is never taken (T=0) and the branch stays blocked 24h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMP comparing the parsed colorspace signature against the literals cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData, logs the constant operand, finds the seed bytes matching the other operand, and substitutes the exact 4-byte FOURCC into the input — naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact 4-byte literal by chance, so they stay blocked all 10 trials.

what_input_feature: An ICC parametric curve whose stored Type selector decodes to the integer 2, so the switch(Type) at line 344 dispatches to case 2 (line 380). The blocker is the true side of the case-2 dispatch; the input must carry a parametricCurveType field exactly equal to 2 somewhere in the parsed profile bytes.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds reach case 2 at runtime (line 380 fires T=602000 in W vs T=0 in L) and the whole case-2 body (lines 380-404) is marked [W] winner-resolving-only, with the branch hit 304270/391450 times by the winners. The cmplog/vpc winners can place the exact byte value 2 into the Type field via I2SRandReplace because the switch(Type) comparison logs the constant operand 2.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds never take case 2: line 380 shows T=0 F=204000 for L, and the entire case-2 body is not marked [L]. naive/value_profile have no I2S substitution to plant the discriminant integer 2 into the Type field, so they stay blocked on this side for the full 24h (avg duration blocked loser=24.00h).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the switch(Type) comparison, finds where the current Type bytes appear in the input, and substitutes the logged constant operand (2) at those offsets, satisfying case 2 directly. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact discriminant by chance, which does not happen in 24h.

what_input_feature: An ICC parametric curve tag whose stored curve-Type integer equals the exact value 3 (the IEC 61966-3 form). The switch(Type) at line 344 must dispatch to case 3: (line 432); no other case arm reaches this branch.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds drive DefaultEvalParametricFn 2,330,000 times and case 3: is taken with W T=694000 vs L T=0, i.e. the winner produces inputs whose decoded Type field is exactly 3; the cmplog/vpc winners discover the matching literal by substituting the logged comparison operand into the input.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds never take case 3: (L T=0 F=204000 at line 432) and resolve=0/10, blocked=10/10; havoc-only mutation of the Type field never lands on the exact integer 3 because there is no coverage gradient guiding it toward that single value.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in both cmplog and value_profile_cmplog, absent in naive and value_profile) intercepts the switch dispatch comparison against the literal 3, logs the operand, and splices it into the input bytes so the Type field decodes to exactly 3; both losers lack I2SRandReplace and cannot solve the equality gate.

what_input_feature: Input bytes parsed into an ICC profile that selects parametric curve Type 4 (case 4, sRGB) and supplies Params[4]/Params[1]/Params[2] coefficients such that for some sample R>=Params[4] the linear form e = Params[1]*R + Params[2] evaluates to <= 0, taking the False side of the e>0 gate at cmsgamma.c:497. The structural precondition is a magic-byte-laden profile header (acsp 'acspAPPL', profile-class/colorspace tags) whose downstream tag table points at a Type-4 parametricCurveType.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning seeds carry the lcms/acsp profile skeleton with valid header magic and varying colorspace tags (RGB/Lab) at offsets 0x14-0x16, and they drive the blocker's enclosing function 941000 times vs 204000 for losers, with the False side actually taken (W F=59900 at line 497 vs L F=0); cmplog/vpc reached this via I2S substitution (dash-row ancestors).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds reach case 4 far less (DefaultEvalParametricFn 204000 invocations) and never take the False side (L 497 F=0): they hit the e>0 True side only, so the non-positive-e configuration of a Type-4 curve is never assembled by plain havoc/CMP_MAP mutation.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog I2SRandReplace substitutes logged CMP operands (the magic/tag-table integers gating the parametric-curve dispatch and the Type-4 selection) directly into the input bytes, assembling the multi-field profile structure that naive havoc and value_profile's Hamming-gradient feedback cannot synthesize, then letting downstream mutation drive e non-positive.

what_input_feature: A tone-curve descriptor in the ICC profile whose parametric-curve Type field equals exactly 5, so the switch(Type) at cmsgamma.c:344 selects the case 5: arm at line 542 instead of any of the other ~22 arms.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winners drive the case 5: true side (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L 542 T=607000 in W vs T=0 in L), having substituted the exact integer 5 into the Type field via I2S; W coverage marks case 5: and its body lines 543-553 as winner-only [W].
why_loser_doesnt: naive/value_profile losers never reach the true side of case 5: (L 542 T=0 F=204000), so they exercise some other Type arm (case 1/4/-4 are [B]) but never produce the discrete Type value 5 within the 10-trial budget.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the switch comparison operand (the constant 5) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and splices it into the input bytes that feed the Type field; the equality jump on a discrete switch constant is exactly the roadblock I2S solves and naive/value_profile (lacking I2S) cannot reliably hit through blind byte mutation. value_profile_cmplog shares the same I2SRandReplace stage, which is why it wins over plain value_profile.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose data-colorspace / PCS FOURCC fields at offsets 0x10-0x17 carry signatures such as 'Lab Lab ' or 'MCH3XYZ ' (rather than 'RGB XYZ '), driving construction of a segmented tone curve (nSegments>0) whose per-segment domain test (R > x0 && R <= x1) fails for every segment, so EvalSegmentedFn runs the descending loop to exhaustion and takes the i<0 (false) loop-exit side at line 721.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'Lab Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) and 'MCH3XYZ ' colorspace signatures at offsets 0x10-0x17; on these the blocker false side is reached (W hits the loop-exit and the line 754 return), with W invoking EvalSegmentedFn 3.82M times vs L 204K and the L 721 row showing F=0.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB XYZ ' (52 47 42 20 / 58 59 5a 20) at offsets 0x10-0x17; these drive a path where every loop iteration either matches a segment domain and returns early or the loop body never falls through, so the false (loop-exit) side of line 721 is never taken (L shows T=204000 F=0 at line 721).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CMP operands recorded when the ICC parser compares the input colorspace FOURCC against expected literals, then substitutes those exact 4-byte signatures into the input at the matching offsets, deterministically producing the colorspace-tagged header naive/value_profile reach only by chance havoc; lineage shows I2S-floor dash rows on both winners.

what_input_feature: The input must be a well-formed ICC profile: literal magic 'lcms'/'acsp' anchors at the header offsets plus a tag table whose entries pass the (offset,size) link check at line 855-856 so two tag descriptors are fetched and compared; the true side of line 706 is taken when those two descriptors have equal ElemCount.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact header magic byte runs ('lcms' at 0x04-0x07, 'acsp'/'APPL' at 0x24-0x2b, XYZ/RGB color-space tokens at 0x10-0x16) intact, which lets _cmsReadHeader parse the tag table and invoke CompatibleTypes; line 706 reaches T=40 (W) vs T=0 (L), i.e. only the winner ever takes the blocked true side.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds corrupt the structural anchors needed downstream — e.g. offset 0x00 carries ff/92/80 and offset 0x17-0x19 carries e8 03 00 (a 0x3e8 size value) instead of the winner's 20 07 d9 — so the tag (offset,size) link check and descriptor lookup never produce a matching descriptor pair, and line 706 stays at T=0 F=543 (false side only).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and splices a logged comparison operand into the matching input offsets. This is what installs the exact header/tag literals (magic, size, color-space fourccs) that naive/value_profile must hit by blind havoc; both winning sides are cmplog/vpc (the two I2S-delta pairs) and their lineage carries I2S-floor dash rows.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte PCS color-space signature in the ICC profile header (offset 0x14-0x17) must be the literal 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) so that cmsGetPCS(hProfile) == cmsSigLabData; any other PCS such as 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) takes the false branch.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries 4c 61 62 20 ('Lab ') at offsets 0x14-0x16, exactly the constant the source compares cmsGetPCS(hProfile) against (cmsSigLabData).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed carries 58 59 5a 20 ('XYZ ') at offsets 0x14-0x16, so cmsGetPCS returns a non-Lab PCS and the true branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the PCS-signature integer/string comparison, log the runtime operand cmsSigLabData, and I2SRandReplace splices that 4-byte literal into the input at the offset where the old PCS value appeared; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate the 4-byte field to the exact magic, which they fail to do in 24h.

source_line: if (cmsIsTag(hProfile, tag16)) { // Is there any LUT-Based table?
what_input_feature: The ICC profile body must declare a tag-table entry whose 4-byte tag signature equals the runtime-selected tag16 (Device2PCS16[Intent], a LUT-based AToBx FOURCC). cmsIsTag walks the parsed tag directory comparing each stored signature against tag16, so the input bytes encoding a directory entry must contain that exact FOURCC.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry valid ICC header bytes (acsp magic, lcms signature, device-class FOURCCs like prtr/spac/mntr and colorspace tags RGB/CMY/Lab/XYZ) and the cmplog winner's ancestor chain shows I2S-floor dash rows, indicating I2SRandReplace spliced the logged tag16 FOURCC into a tag-table offset so the stored signature matches.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive / value_profile) all show the false side: at line 355 L takes T=0 F=48, i.e. no stored tag signature ever equals tag16. Their havoc/token mutators never reconstruct the precise 4-byte FOURCC needed in the tag directory, so cmsIsTag stays false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog intercept the integer/string CMP inside cmsIsTag's signature comparison, log the expected tag16 FOURCC, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact 4-byte value into the input at a tag-directory offset. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot land the exact magic, so they stay blocked.

what_input_feature: A valid ICC profile header (lcms/acsp magic) whose 4-byte profile-connection-space (PCS) signature at offset 0x14-0x16 spells 'Lab' rather than 'XYZ'. The Lab PCS routes lcms to build a pipeline that contains an MPE stage whose DupElemPtr function pointer is NULL, so cmsStageDup at cmslut.c:1258 takes the (globally-blocked) false branch and executes NewMPE->Data = NULL.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the bytes 4c 61 62 ('Lab') at offsets 0x14-0x16 and the false-branch-only lines 1270-1271 (NewMPE->Data = NULL) are marked [W]; the winner resolves the branch in 10/10 trials with hitcount 82 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: All five loser-blocking seeds carry 58 59 5a ('XYZ') at offsets 0x14-0x16, an XYZ PCS that builds only stages whose DupElemPtr is non-NULL; the loser blocks 10/10 trials with hitcount 0 and never reaches line 1270.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer where lcms's signature-field comparison logged the operands, then substitutes the matching operand bytes (the 'Lab' PCS string) into the input at the offset where the seed already held the comparand. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble on the exact 4-byte 'Lab' substitution by blind havoc byte mutation alone, which never lands in 24h.

what_input_feature: A valid ICC color-space FOURCC signature in the InputFormat/OutputFormat header fields (offsets 0x10-0x16: e.g. 'Lab '/'Lab ' rather than 'RGB '/'XYZ ') such that _cmsICCcolorSpace((int)T_COLORSPACE(*InputFormat)) maps to a recognized cmsColorSpaceSignature != 0, so the line-668 check (ColorSpace == 0 || OutputColorSpace == 0) is FALSE and execution continues into resampling.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact color-space token bytes the equality CMPs in _cmsICCcolorSpace expect; cmplog/vpc logged the constant operand of each color-space comparison and I2SRandReplace substituted it into the header offsets, producing seeds whose ColorSpace lookup succeeds (avg hitcount on the false side 53/71, resolved 10/10).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry header bytes ('RGB '/'XYZ ' at 0x10-0x16) that, in this trial population, drive the lookup to a value that fails the line-668 guard so they take the TRUE side and return FALSE early; naive/value_profile have no I2S substitution to plant the matching color-space FOURCC and stay blocked 10/10 for 24h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog both add I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the color-space comparison operands, and splices the matching FOURCC into the input header. naive and plain value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they cannot place the exact color-space token by directed substitution. This is the I2S roadblock-solving mechanism, matching claimed_mechanism I2SRandReplace.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile carrying a tone-curve (Curve1) whose cmsEvalToneCurveFloat output for at least one of the 256 samples is >= 131072.0, so the comparison `if (y < 131072.0)` takes its false side. Reaching this requires a well-formed profile header (`acsp`/`mntr` magic, valid colorspace tag e.g. Lab/XYZ) plus curve-tag bytes whose decoded float magnitude is enormous.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach the curve evaluation and take the false branch 5580/5599 times (W shows F=5580 on the blocker), and the BYTE DIFF shows winners carrying alternate colorspace/tag bytes (4c 'L', 61 'a', 62 'b' at offsets 0x14-0x16) and a `63 'c'` at 0x2c absent in losers, i.e. a distinct parsed-profile configuration that drives the curve into the large-output regime.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds execute the curve evaluation but the false side is never taken (L shows T=15300 F=0 on the blocker); their bytes hold only the unmodified `58 'X' 59 'Y' 5a 'Z'` colorspace and zeros at the differentiating offsets, so the tone curve always evaluates below 131072.0.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver and substitutes one comparison operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting the exact tag/curve bytes whose decoded float clears the 131072.0 magnitude threshold; the two winners (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) both add I2S over their non-I2S sibling (naive, value_profile), so the divergence is attributable to the I2S technique delta.

what_input_feature: The profile colorspace/PCS signature bytes at input offsets 0x10-0x16 must encode a value whose derived InputFormat passes _cmsFormatterIs8bit. Winners carry the 4-byte ASCII run 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at both 0x10 and 0x14; losers carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20). The blocker on line 1676 demands an 8-bit InputFormat, which only the winner's colorspace fields produce.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds hold 4c 61 62 (Lab) at offsets 0x10-0x12 and 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF shows 0x10=4c, 0x11=61, 0x12=62, 0x14=4c, 0x15=61, 0x16=62), and the cmplog/vpc winners discovered the branch in 1.3-2.3h, resolving it in 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 53-71 on the true side.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hold 52 47 42 (RGB) at 0x10-0x12 and 58 59 5a (XYZ) at 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF DIFFER rows), so InputFormat is not 8-bit; line 1676 takes the false (return FALSE) side in all 10 trials, blocked 24.00h with hitcount 0 on the true side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMPs inside _cmsFormatterIs8bit's format derivation, logs the expected 8-bit-formatter operand, finds the colorspace byte run in the input and substitutes the matching value at offsets 0x10-0x16. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never planted the exact colorspace bytes that yield an 8-bit InputFormat.

what_input_feature: The ICC profile header's data-colorspace/PCS fields at offsets 0x10-0x16 must hold the four-char signatures 'Lab ' (0x4c 0x61 0x62) for both colorspace and PCS, instead of 'RGB '/'XYZ '. These bytes drive Type/channel computation so dwInput no longer equals (dwInput & ~Mask)==Type for any of the matching InputFormatters16 entries, causing the loop at L3516 to run to completion and take the blocked false side (loop exit, break at L3525, then fall through to L3543).
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 4c 61 62 20 (Lab ) at offsets 0x10-0x12 and again at 0x14-0x16, a colorspace pairing that does not satisfy the L3519 equality for any 16-bit input formatter, so the for-loop scans all entries (W: T=900 F=20) and the false/exit side is reached.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 (RGB ) at 0x10-0x12 and 58 59 5a 20 (XYZ ) at 0x14-0x16; this colorspace matches an InputFormatters16 entry at L3519, so the loop returns early (L3520-3522) before ever exhausting the entries (L: T=340 F=0), never taking the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the L3519 comparison's operands (the table entry's Type and the runtime-derived dwInput, plus the underlying 4-char signature compares in cmsGetColorSpace/header parsing) are logged, locates the matching colorspace bytes in the input, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets — flipping the signature toward a value that misses every InputFormatters16 row and forces the loop-exit (false) side. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; value_profile's CMP_MAP only rewards Hamming-closer operands, which biases toward MATCHING a formatter (taking true) rather than exhaustively missing all of them, so neither loser ever reaches the false side in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte data-colorspace signature at offset 0x10-0x13 of the ICC header must be a non-RGB FOURCC (e.g. 'Lab ', 'CMY ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4', 'XYZ') rather than 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20), so _cmsEndPointsBySpace's switch on Space does not enter the cmsSigRgbData case and instead reaches the Lab/CMYK/CMY/default arms, taking the FALSE (non-RGB) side of the blocker at cmspcs.c:732.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-RGB colorspace tags at offset 0x10-0x13: 'CMY ', 'Lab ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4' (W bytes at 0x10 are 4c/43/4d/35 = L/C/M/5, never the loser's uniform 52='R'), so the dispatch lands on a later case arm and exits the RGB arm via its false side.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has the colorspace field fixed to 'RGB ' (offset 0x10-0x13 = 52 47 42 20, 20/20 occurrences), so Space == cmsSigRgbData always holds, the RGB switch arm is entered (true side), and the blocked false side at cmspcs.c:732 is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/string CMP that dispatches the switch on the colorspace signature, log the runtime operand (the cmsSig* FOURCC constants such as cmsSigLabData/cmsSigCmykData) into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes those logged constants into the input at the offset where the current 'RGB ' tag appears. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate a 4-byte field into a valid alternate FOURCC, which never happens in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature field of the ICC profile header (input offsets 0x10-0x16, rendered 'Lab ') must spell the Lab signature so T_COLORSPACE decodes to PT_Lab and the switch in _cmsICCcolorSpace selects case PT_Lab at cmspcs.c:781; loser inputs carrying 'RGB ' / 'XYZ ' fall through to non-Lab cases.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal bytes 4c 61 62 20 ('Lab ') at offsets 0x10-0x13 (and again at 0x14-0x16), so the parsed colorspace decodes to PT_Lab and the true side of the case PT_Lab branch is taken (W exclusively hits source line 781).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed instead holds 52 47 42 20 / 58 59 5a 20 ('RGB ' / 'XYZ ') at those same offsets, so the switch never reaches PT_Lab; L takes the default arm at line 805 and leaves line 781 unhit (T=0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the equality comparison of the parsed colorspace tag against the Lab constant, logs both operands, and splices the Lab signature bytes into the input at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so they must blindly mutate a 4-byte ASCII tag (effectively a magic-value match) by chance, which never happens in 10 trials. The mechanism is identical for both decisive pairs, hence single_feature.

This is input-to-state substitution.

what_input_feature: The input must begin with a well-formed sfnt/TrueType font header (00 01 00 00 sfnt version) followed by a table directory whose entries carry recognized 4-byte ASCII table tags such as 'GSUB' or 'GPOS' at fixed offsets, so the recursive sanitize walk reaches check_range with a non-degenerate len and a base inside [start,end].
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the constant sfnt header bytes 00 01 00 00 and embeds the literal ASCII tags GSUB/GPOS/GPOS at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the exact 4-byte table tags the sanitize code memcmp's against; this drives the structure-traversal that invokes check_range ~110000 times and takes the false branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are tiny (6-35 bytes), lack the sfnt header constant and any 4-byte table tag at the magic offsets (their offset 0x0c-0x0f bytes are 00/space/random), so the table directory never validates and check_range is reached only ~20 times, never on the resolving path.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer recording the operands of the tag/version comparisons, scans the input for the matching operand and substitutes the target constant (sfnt version, GSUB/GPOS tags) directly at the right offset; naive and plain value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reliably synthesize these 4-byte magic constants by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: The IPv6 destination field parsed from the message must equal the link-local or realm-local all-thread-nodes multicast address. In the winning seeds this surfaces as a tightly-concentrated anchor byte run at offsets 0x1a-0x28 (33 00 40 fd de ad 00 be ef 00 ... 01), the exact constant operand the equality compare at line 82 tests against.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winning-resolving seed carries the same fixed byte run at offsets 0x1a-0x28; the BYTE DIFF shows W is perfectly concentrated there (e.g. 0x1c=40(@)x19, 0x1d=fd(.)x19, 0x1e=de(.)x19, 0x1f=ad(.)x19, 0x21=be(.)x19, 0x22=ef(.)x19), meaning all 19 winner samples share that constant, matching the compared address.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have no such concentration at those offsets — they scatter (e.g. 0x1c shows 00/78/30/c4 +6u, 0x1e shows 5c/00/30/ef +5u tagged DIFFER), so the destination never equals the all-thread-nodes address and the equality compare stays False, blocking the True side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/memory compare at line 82 into the CmpLogObserver buffer, then I2SRandReplace scans the input for one operand and substitutes the other, planting the exact all-thread-nodes address constant into the destination field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the multi-byte address by blind havoc, which they never do in 10 trials.
